description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Create a new infectious-disease research project, then find COVID-19 datasets collected from 2020 onward and request 12‑month access to the one with the largest sample size, linking the request to that project.
tasks.name: Create a new infectious-disease research project, then find COVID-19 datasets collected from 2020 onward and request 12‑month access to the one with the largest sample size, linking the request to that project.
tasks.description: Create a new infectious-disease research project, then find COVID-19 datasets collected from 2020 onward and request 12‑month access to the one with the largest sample size, linking the request to that project.

tasks.instruction: Find mental health datasets with sample sizes between 1,000 and 5,000 participants, sort them alphabetically by title, and add the first three matching datasets to your favorites.
tasks.name: Find mental health datasets with sample sizes between 1,000 and 5,000 participants, sort them alphabetically by title, and add the first three matching datasets to your favorites.
tasks.description: Find mental health datasets with sample sizes between 1,000 and 5,000 participants, sort them alphabetically by title, and add the first three matching datasets to your favorites.

tasks.instruction: Create a new diabetes-focused workspace, then in a national diabetes registry dataset build a cohort query for U.S. participants aged 40–65 with type 2 diabetes and at least one HbA1c lab result, and save the query.
tasks.name: Create a new diabetes-focused workspace, then in a national diabetes registry dataset build a cohort query for U.S. participants aged 40–65 with type 2 diabetes and at least one HbA1c lab result, and save the query.

tasks.instruction: From your active projects that expire within the next month, identify the one with the earliest expiration date and submit a 3‑month access extension request with a brief justification.
tasks.name: From your active projects that expire within the next month, identify the one with the earliest expiration date and submit a 3‑month access extension request with a brief justification.
tasks.description: From your active projects that expire within the next month, identify the one with the earliest expiration date and submit a 3‑month access extension request with a brief justification.

tasks.instruction: In your cardiology outcomes project, add an existing collaborator with a data analyst–type role and configure their permissions so they can view but not edit data exports.
tasks.name: In your cardiology outcomes project, add an existing collaborator with a data analyst–type role and configure their permissions so they can view but not edit data exports.
tasks.description: In your cardiology outcomes project, add an existing collaborator with a data analyst–type role and configure their permissions so they can view but not edit data exports.

tasks.instruction: Search for controlled-access breast cancer genomics datasets with more than 200 variables and request 6‑month access to the dataset with the highest variable count, linking it to a new oncology project.
tasks.name: Search for controlled-access breast cancer genomics datasets with more than 200 variables and request 6‑month access to the dataset with the highest variable count, linking it to a new oncology project.
tasks.description: Search for controlled-access breast cancer genomics datasets with more than 200 variables and request 6‑month access to the dataset with the highest variable count, linking it to a new oncology project.

tasks.instruction: Create a new data use agreement by cloning the primary clinical DUA template, change the data retention period to 24 months, link it to your COVID-19 outcomes project, and activate the agreement.
tasks.name: Create a new data use agreement by cloning the primary clinical DUA template, change the data retention period to 24 months, link it to your COVID-19 outcomes project, and activate the agreement.
tasks.description: Create a new data use agreement by cloning the primary clinical DUA template, change the data retention period to 24 months, link it to your COVID-19 outcomes project, and activate the agreement.

tasks.instruction: Within an approved diabetes registry dataset in one of your projects, configure a new secure export job that limits output to 5,000 records, sorts by patient ID in ascending order, and runs weekly on Mondays.
tasks.name: Within an approved diabetes registry dataset in one of your projects, configure a new secure export job that limits output to 5,000 records, sorts by patient ID in ascending order, and runs weekly on Mondays.

tasks.instruction: Find open-access asthma datasets from Canada with collection starting in 2016 or later, sort them by sample size, and pin the two largest datasets to your dashboard.
tasks.name: Find open-access asthma datasets from Canada with collection starting in 2016 or later, sort them by sample size, and pin the two largest datasets to your dashboard.
